role_preferences.recent_work_context: Training machine learning models based on onboarding graph linkage vector features.
role_preferences.anticipated_start_date_preference: One month out from the current date
role_preferences.location_and_relocation_rule: The candidate is open to relocate anywhere in the United States. If the form presents a checkbox list of multiple locations to select from, select ALL of them.

work_experience.role: Associate Fraud Risk Strategy Data Scientist
work_experience.location: 6220 America Center Dr Ste 100, San Jose, CA 95002
work_experience.description: Engineered a multi-objective NSGA-II genetic algorithm via Optuna to jointly optimize 15 production rules, processing 15,000 trials in 2 minutes and reducing manual referrals by 4.4% per quarter. Prototyped a KYC fraud detection system combining a Random Forest model (0.79 AUC), a NetworkX identity graph utilizing vectorized identity signals, and an agentic AI LiteLLM tool-calling loop to synthesize investigation tickets. Deployed an end-to-end AWS SageMaker batch ML pipeline utilizing graph-based fanout, achieving 99.5% recall and 94.5% precision on reachable fraud. Identified and resolved a portfolio-wide pipeline bug affecting 411,000 payment events across a 6 month window, enabling clean rule re-tuning. Developed automated KPI dashboards with Tableau, providing stakeholders with real-time visibility into fraud prevention trends, rule performance week-over-week, and loss prevention metrics.

Fine-tuned ChatGPT models for cryo-electron microscopy (cryoEM) workstations to improve AI-assisted scientific imaging workflows. Led the integration of AI capabilities into the company website, enabling direct user interaction with fine-tuned models for cryo-EM data analysis tasks.

Engineered and rigorously tested data science workflows tailored to various industry sectors, enhancing team insights into workstation performance. Conducted extensive benchmarking of workflows by simulating real-world industry use cases using Python and Scikit-Learn, improving efficiency by 20%.

routing_priorities.contextual_citizenship_and_export_rules: Determine whether the job listing is based in the US or Canada. If the role is located in the US, select 'United States of America (USA)' for citizenship and export control fields. If the role is located in Canada, act entirely as a Canadian native: select 'Canada' for citizenship and 'Canada' for export licensing country fields.
us_location_priority_ladder: Bay Area (San Jose, San Francisco, Milpitas)
Anywhere else in the United States
routing_priorities.location_selection_execution_rule: When resolving a relocation or location drop-down field: 1. Evaluate if any option in the webpage drop-down matches the exact city/location stated in the active job listing page text. If a match exists, prioritize and select that option. 2. If the listing location is not an option, use the corresponding country ladder (US or Canada) to find and select the highest available fallback match present in the webpage options list.

compensation_rules.range_midpoint_rule: If the job description lists an explicit salary range corresponding to the primary target location (prioritizing the San Francisco / California tier if multiple states are listed), calculate the exact mathematical midpoint between the minimum and maximum salary boundary numbers. Return that calculated midpoint value formatted as a rounded integer.

regulatory_self_identification.veteran_status_selection: I am not a protected veteran
regulatory_self_identification.disability_status_rule: Select 'Yes, I have a disability, or have had one in the past' because standard regulatory forms (Form CC-305) explicitly include conditions like depression and ADHD within their self-identification guidelines.
